title: Cybele laurina
intro: Cybele laurina, also known as wild cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, coughs, and colds, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cybele laurina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Cybele laurina is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in cough syrups and lozenges to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
